name: Hazelnut Cappucino Torte
recipeIngredient: 8 oz. semisweet chocolate, cut into pieces
1 cup toasted hazelnuts or almonds, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan, melt semisweet and milk chocolate with whipping cream and instant coffee crystals over low heat, stirring constantly until melted. Allow to cool to room temperature.
2. In a large mixing bowl, beat eggs, coffee liqueur (or coffee), and vanilla extract on low speed with an electric mixer until combined. Gradually add flour and sugar, beating on medium to high speed for 8 minutes. The batter should be light and slightly thickened. Stir in about one-fourth of the chocolate mixture.
3. Gently fold the remaining chocolate mixture into the egg mixture, then fold in chopped hazelnuts (or almonds).
4. Grease and flour a 9-inch springform pan. Pour the batter into the pan and bake at 325°F for 40 to 45 minutes or until puffed around the outer edge but still slightly soft in the center. Let cool on a wire rack for 20 minutes.
5. Remove the sides of the pan and let cool completely. Cover tightly and refrigerate up to 24 hours.
6. To serve, allow the torte to stand at room temperature for 30 minutes before serving. Top with Mocha Cream and optional coffee beans.
